title: Personalised Encounter Journey
description: When a patient presents, the engine matches their clinical context against active pathways and produces a tailored step-by-step encounter plan with no modifications needed.

title: Multi-morbidity Pathway Reconciliation
description: Patient triggers multiple pathways simultaneously. The engine detects conflicts between condition-specific treatments and reconciles them into a single coherent plan.

title: Safety Guardrail Enforcement
description: Before any activity reaches a clinician, the engine checks contraindications, allergy conflicts, critical lab thresholds, and drug interactions.

title: Urgency Escalation
description: Patient context triggers urgency elevation — sepsis with high lactate, immunocompromised status, or active foot ulcer with poor glycaemic control.

title: Specialist Referral Routing
description: The engine determines which specialist the patient needs and adds appropriate referral activities based on comorbidity patterns.

title: Contraindication-Driven Medication Adjustment
description: The engine flags medications that are contraindicated for this patient and adds appropriate alternatives or specialist referral.

title: Monitoring Schedule Personalisation
description: Monitoring frequency and follow-up approach are adapted based on frailty, recurrent admissions, and transport barriers.

title: Polypharmacy Medication Review
description: Patients on multiple medications have medication review prioritised before any treatment changes.

title: Language and Social Barrier Adaptation
description: Encounter journey is adapted for patients with language barriers or transport difficulties.

title: Maternal Health — Advanced Age Monitoring
description: Pregnant patients aged 40+ receive enhanced antenatal monitoring with increased scan frequency and consultant involvement.

title: Maternal Health — Pre-eclampsia Prevention
description: Patients with pre-eclampsia history receive aspirin prophylaxis from 12 weeks and increased BP monitoring.

title: Sepsis — Immunocompromised Escalation
description: Immunocompromised patients with suspected sepsis receive broader antimicrobial coverage and lower red-flag thresholds.

title: Sepsis — Allergy-Safe Antimicrobial Selection
description: Patients with penicillin allergy receive alternative antimicrobial regimens for sepsis management.

title: Sepsis — Heart Failure Fluid Safety
description: Patients with heart failure and sepsis receive cautious fluid resuscitation to avoid pulmonary oedema.

title: Governance — Pathway Currency Checking
description: Automated detection of overdue reviews, superseded source guidelines, missing clinical owners, and provenance gaps.

title: Audit Trail — Decision Explainability
description: Every personalisation decision is logged with full provenance, modifications, safety warnings, and confidence assessment.

title: Deviation Register — Standard vs Individualised
description: When individualisation changes anything in the standard clinical pathway, the deviation is explicitly documented showing the standard step, the individualised step, the reason, and the evidence reference.

title: COPD — Frequent Exacerbator Management
description: COPD patients with 2+ admissions in 12 months receive enhanced management including self-management plans and rescue packs.
